mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in initial unemployment claims above 250,000 signals a weakening labor market that reduces consumer spending and corporate profitability, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with rising unemployment claims are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in industrial production below 100 signals a weakening supply chain that reduces corporate profitability and equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in periods with declining industrial production are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in real GDP above 3% signals a strengthening economy that increases corporate profitability and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with rising real GDP are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in the federal funds rate and a 2-month decline in the 10-year Treasury yield signals a dovish monetary policy stance that reduces discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in periods with dovish monetary policy are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in initial unemployment claims above 250,000 and a 2-month increase in the unemployment rate above 4% signals a weakening labor market that reduces consumer spending and corporate profitability, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with weakening labor market are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in industrial production below 100 and a 2-month decline in the housing market index below 50 signals a weakening supply chain and housing market that reduces corporate profitability and equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in periods with weakening supply chain and housing market are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in real GDP above 3% and a 2-month increase in real disposable personal income above 2% signals a strengthening economy that increases corporate profitability and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods with strengthening economy are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p < 0.05).
